1482:
WITH YearlyConsumption AS (
    SELECT 
        c.Segment,
        SUM(CASE WHEN SUBSTR(y.Date, 1, 4) = '2013' THEN y.Consumption ELSE 0 END) AS Consumption2013,
        SUM(CASE WHEN SUBSTR(y.Date, 1, 4) = '2012' THEN y.Consumption ELSE 0 END) AS Consumption2012
    FROM customers c
    JOIN yearmonth y ON c.CustomerID = y.CustomerID
    WHERE c.Currency = 'EUR'
    GROUP BY c.Segment
),
PercentageChange AS (
    SELECT 
        Segment,
        ((Consumption2013 - Consumption2012) / Consumption2013) * 100 AS PercentageChange
    FROM YearlyConsumption
)
SELECT 
    Segment,
    CASE 
        WHEN PercentageChange > 0 THEN 'Increase'
        ELSE 'Decrease'
    END AS ChangeType,
    ABS(PercentageChange) AS AbsolutePercentageChange
FROM PercentageChange
ORDER BY AbsolutePercentageChange DESC;

WITH MonthlyConsumption AS (
    SELECT 
        ym.CustomerID,
        SUM(ym.Consumption) AS TotalConsumption
    FROM 
        yearmonth ym
    WHERE 
        SUBSTR(ym.Date, 1, 4) = '2012' AND SUBSTR(ym.Date, 5, 2) = '02'
    GROUP BY 
        ym.CustomerID
),
HighConsumers AS (
    SELECT 
        mc.CustomerID
    FROM 
        MonthlyConsumption mc
    WHERE 
        mc.TotalConsumption > 528.3
)
SELECT 
    CAST(COUNT(hc.CustomerID) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(DISTINCT c.CustomerID) AS PercentageOfCustomersWithHighConsumption
FROM 
    customers c
LEFT JOIN 
    HighConsumers hc ON c.CustomerID = hc.CustomerID;

-- Step 1: Find the maximum hemoglobin count
WITH MaxHemoglobin AS (
    SELECT MAX(HGB) AS max_hgb
    FROM Laboratory
),

-- Step 2: Join Patient and Laboratory tables and calculate age
PatientAgeWithMaxHemoglobin AS (
    SELECT 
        P.ID,
        STRFTIME('%Y', E.`Examination Date`) - STRFTIME('%Y', P.Birthday) AS AgeAtExamination,
        P.Diagnosis,
        L.HGB
    FROM Patient P
    INNER JOIN Laboratory L ON P.ID = L.ID
    INNER JOIN Examination E ON P.ID = E.ID
    WHERE L.HGB = (SELECT max_hgb FROM MaxHemoglobin)
)

-- Step 3: Select the required columns from the filtered result
SELECT 
    AgeAtExamination,
    Diagnosis
FROM PatientAgeWithMaxHemoglobin;

WITH LowestConsumption AS (
    SELECT 
        c.Segment,
        ym.CustomerID,
        SUM(ym.Consumption) AS TotalConsumption
    FROM 
        customers c
    JOIN 
        yearmonth ym ON c.CustomerID = ym.CustomerID
    WHERE 
        SUBSTR(ym.Date, 1, 4) = '2013'
    GROUP BY 
        c.Segment, ym.CustomerID
),
RankedCustomers AS (
    SELECT 
        Segment,
        CustomerID,
        TotalConsumption,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY Segment ORDER BY TotalConsumption ASC) AS Rank
    FROM 
        LowestConsumption
)
SELECT 
    Segment,
    CustomerID,
    TotalConsumption
FROM 
    RankedCustomers
WHERE 
    Rank = 1;

    SELECT 
        SUBSTR(y.Date, 1, 4) || '-' || SUBSTR(y.Date, 5, 2) AS Month,
        SUM(y.Consumption) AS TotalConsumption

        customers c
    JOIN 
        yearmonth y ON c.CustomerID = y.CustomerID
    WHERE 
        c.Segment = 'SME'
        AND y.Date LIKE '2013%'
    GROUP BY 
        Month
),
RankedMonthlyConsumption AS (
    SELECT 
        Month,
        TotalConsumption,
        RANK() OVER (ORDER BY TotalConsumption DESC) AS ConsumptionRank
    FROM 
        MonthlyConsumption
)
SELECT 
    Month
FROM 
    RankedMonthlyConsumption
WHERE 
    ConsumptionRank = 1;

        COUNT(*) AS BadgeCount

        Year

    SELECT 
        SUM(BadgeCount) AS TotalBadgeCount
    FROM 
        StudentBadges
),
YearlyPercentages AS (
    SELECT 
        sb.Year,
        sb.BadgeCount,
        tb.TotalBadgeCount,
        (sb.BadgeCount * 100.0 / tb.TotalBadgeCount) AS Percentage
    FROM 
        StudentBadges sb
    CROSS JOIN

WITH FemaleClients AS (
    SELECT c.client_id, c.district_id, c.birth_date
    FROM client c
    WHERE c.gender = 'F'
),
OldestFemaleClient AS (
    SELECT fc.client_id, fc.district_id, fc.birth_date
    FROM FemaleClients fc
    ORDER BY fc.birth_date ASC
    LIMIT 1
),
AverageSalaryOfOldestFemale AS (
    SELECT d.A11 AS avg_salary
    FROM OldestFemaleClient ofc
    JOIN district d ON ofc.district_id = d.district_id
),
HighestAndLowestSalaries AS (
    SELECT MAX(d.A11) AS max_avg_salary, MIN(d.A11) AS min_avg_salary
    FROM district d
),
SalaryGap AS (
    SELECT hls.max_avg_salary - hls.min_avg_salary AS salary_gap
    FROM HighestAndLowestSalaries hls
)
SELECT 
    ofc.client_id AS account_number,
    asof.avg_salary,
    sg.salary_gap
FROM OldestFemaleClient ofc
JOIN AverageSalaryOfOldestFemale asof ON 1=1
CROSS JOIN SalaryGap sg;
